questions.question_text: What is your overall impression of the health care system in Canada?
What is your overall impression of the health care system in the United States?
For you personally, do you think that the American health care system is better, worse or much the same as the Canadian health care system?
Overall, in your view, which health care system is the most effective, the Canadian or the American?
And which one is fairest in your view, the Canadian or the American?
From what you know, would you say that the proportion of the population unable to afford the care it needs in Canada is higher, lower or about the same as in the United States?
And would you say that the proportion of the population unable to receive the most effective drugs in Canada is higher, lower or about the same as in the United States?
And would you say that the proportion of the population unable to receive high quality medical care in Canada is higher, lower or about the same as in the United States?
And do you think that the proportion of the population that had to wait longer than reasonable to get health care services in Canada is higher, lower or about the same as in the United States?

As you may know, currently Canada's health care system is a mixed public and private system. What are the proportions of health expenditures being financed today by the public sector and by the private sector respectively?

response_options.label: 90% financed by the public sector; 10% financed by the priva
80% financed by the public sector; 20% financed by the priva
70% financed by the public sector; 30% financed by the priva
60% financed by the public sector; 40% financed by the priva

questions.question_text: As you may know, currently the United States' health care system is a mixed private and public system. What are the proportions of health expenditures being financed today by the private sector and by the public sector respectively?

75% financed by the private sector; 25% financed by the publ
55% financed by the private sector; 45% financed by the publ
35% financed by the private sector; 65% financed by the publ

In your opinion, should the private sector's involvement in delivering health care to Canadians be larger, smaller or about the same as it is now?
In your opinion, should the government's involvement in delivering health care to Americans be larger, smaller or about the same as it is now?
Overall, do you think that more private sector involvement would lead to improvement in: / the quality of healthcare services offered in Canada
Overall, do you think that more private sector involvement would lead to improvement in: / access to healthcare services
Overall, do you think that more private sector involvement would lead to improvement in: / the overall cost of healthcare?
Overall, do you think that more government involvement would lead to improvement in: / the quality of healthcare services offered in America
Overall, do you think that more government involvement would lead to improvement in: / access to healthcare services
Overall, do you think that more government involvement would lead to improvement in: / the overall cost of healthcare?
Our society as a whole would be worse-off if a two-tier health care system were to be adopted in Canada.
Working families would be worse-off if a two-tier health care system were to be adopted in Canada.
I personally would be worse-off if a two-tier health care system were to be adopted in Canada.
Our society as a whole would be worse-off if a publicly-funded health care system were to be adopted in America.
Working families would be worse-off if a publicly-funded health care system were to be adopted in America.
I personally would be worse-off if a publicly-funded health care system were to be adopted in America.
Universal publicly-funded health care is part of what it means to be Canadian and reflects our core values.
Market-based health care is part of what it means to be American and reflects our core values.
If you had to choose, would you rather have a universally free and accessible health care system or a universally free and accessible education system?

response_options.label: Universally free and accessible health care system
Universally free and accessible education system
